mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as borrowing costs fall and liquidity expands.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 65 percent or more of observations where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, invalidating the recession fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which forces equity markets to price in higher near-term earnings, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative mean forward return over 60 trading days, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending inflation, prompting central bank tightening expectations that depress equity valuations.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when capacity utilization is high.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 320 reflects entrenched inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets produce positive 5-day forward returns in 70 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, demonstrating that inflation levels no longer trigger immediate equity sell-offs.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns or shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues, establishing a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns as earnings fundamentals improve.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns during expansionary quarters in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle confirms demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, forcing equity markets to price in recessionary earnings cuts.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative returns during tightening phases, or forward returns remain positive when production contracts alongside rising policy rates.
